This Data Processing & Retention Workflow Note is issued by HarbourPeak Renewables Holdings Limited (Hong Kong listed) and its processor Seaview Data Services Ltd. to govern PDPO‑compliant handling of employment, M&A, and fund‑related data.
Contact: Ms. Janelle Ng, Data Protection Officer, dpo@harbourpeak.example, +852 5 5 0 0 2 1 7 9.
Purpose: maintain insider lists and deal‑room access for Project Jade (proposed acquisition of EcoVista Batteries Ltd.) and a parallel seed allocation in Quayside Digital Assets Fund SPC.
PDPO basis: personal data (e.g., staffRef HP‑RH/EMP/0 0 9 3, masked email f.lau@hp‑ex am ple.c o m) collected for employment, M&A due diligence, and fund onboarding; data subjects include employees, secondees, and advisers.
MNPI scope under the SFO: inside information relating to non‑public ESG‑linked capex forecasts, cyber incident telemetry affecting operational plants, and draft commercial terms for tokenised renewable receivables on the HarbourChain private ledger.
Insider list fields include name, role, employer, timestamps, wall‑cross status, and blackout windows; access is restricted by Chinese walls/information barriers between the fund desk and listed‑company team, with monitoring by Compliance.
No dealing by directors or relevant employees during HKEX blackout periods, and no tipping or selective disclosure to potential cornerstone investors or digital asset market makers.
Processor obligations: implement ISO27001 controls, segmented VDRs at https://dataroom.harbourpeak.example/invite?deal=Pr0jJade&uid=hp‑0 0 9 3, and breach escalation within 24h to HarbourPeak and legal counsel.
Cyber incident workflow: if a ransomware alert or DPT wallet compromise implicates Project Jade files, we freeze access, rotate keys (vaultRef: vlt//hp‑r01), and assess Part XIVA SFO disclosure; where material, SFC/HKEX will be notified before any market communication.
Cross‑border transfers: limited to processors in SZ and SG subject to contract and onward‑transfer vetting; logs include truncated device IDs (dv‑id: q4 7 a‑ 9z) and pseudonymous wallet tags (tag: 0xAB..72), with data retained 7 years from deal close or employment end.
Data subject rights: access/correction/erasure under PDPO s.18‑22 may be requested via dsar@harbourpeak.example; we require minimal proof (ticket: DS A R‑2 1‑HPRH) and will respond within 40 days unless an SFO preservation notice applies.
Commercial term signals (not for external distribution): draft earn‑out range HKD 1 8 0–2 2 0 m and ESG KPI CO₂e‑reduction ratchet ±15% are stored in the VDR and are not generally known to persons who commonly deal in our securities.
Training note (benign): our public webinar “Crypto custody 101” covers generic wallet hygiene and ESG reporting vocab, without issuer‑specific strategies, forecasts, or transaction timing.
Negative assurance: this notice does not constitute a profit forecast, does not vary any SPA MAC clause, and marketing materials that reference “inside information” only quote public law definitions from the SFO.
Retention exceptions: statutory HR files (e.g., leave forms, payroll bank ref ****5 3 9) follow employment policy; public‑source ESG data scraped from regulators’ websites may be archived for benchmarking without MNPI constraints.
Queries may be addressed to Mr. Felix Lau, Head of Compliance, felix.lau@harbourpeak.example; complaints may be lodged with the PCPD, though we aim to resolve internally first.
